Student.title: Student
Student.description: Represents a student user registered on the Al-Bashmohandes platform.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Student entity.

name.description: The full name of the student.

email.description: The email address of the student, used for login (authentication details are managed externally).

parentPhoneNumber.description: The phone number of the student's parent or guardian.

academicYear.description: The student's current academic year (e.g., 'First Year High School', 'Second Year High School', 'Third Year High School').

registrationDate.description: The date and time when the student account was registered.

lastLoginDate.description: The date and time of the student's last successful login.

AdminUser.title: AdminUser
AdminUser.description: Represents an administrator user of the Al-Bashmohandes platform.

id.description: Unique identifier for the AdminUser entity.

name.description: The full name of the administrator.

email.description: The email address of the administrator, used for login (authentication details are managed externally).

registrationDate.description: The date and time when the admin account was created.

lastLoginDate.description: The date and time of the admin's last successful login.

Course.title: Course
Course.description: Represents an educational course or a standalone exam/quiz offered on the platform.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Course entity.

title.description: The title of the course or standalone exam.

description.description: A detailed description of the course content, objectives, or exam scope.

price.description: The price of the course or standalone exam in the local currency.

targetAcademicYear.description: The academic year(s) for which this course is primarily targeted.

createdAt.description: The date and time when the course was first created.

updatedAt.description: The date and time when the course was last updated.

uploadedByAdminUserId.description: Reference to the AdminUser who initially uploaded or created this course. (Relationship: AdminUser 1:N Course)

CourseContent.title: CourseContent
CourseContent.description: Represents an individual piece of content within a Course, such as a video, quiz, or exam.

id.description: Unique identifier for the CourseContent entity.

courseId.description: Reference to the Course this content item belongs to. (Relationship: Course 1:N CourseContent)

title.description: The title of the content item (e.g., 'Introduction to Algebra Video', 'Chapter 1 Quiz').

description.description: A brief description or summary of the content item.

contentType.description: The type of content, indicating if it's a video, quiz, or exam. ('Video', 'Quiz', 'Exam').

orderIndex.description: The sequential order of this content item within its parent course.

youtubeLink.description: The YouTube URL for video content. This property is present only if 'contentType' is 'Video'.

durationInSeconds.description: The duration of the video content in seconds. This property is present only if 'contentType' is 'Video'.

passMarkPercentage.description: The minimum percentage score required to 'pass' this quiz or exam. This property is present only if 'contentType' is 'Quiz' or 'Exam'.

allowInstantResultsDisplay.description: Indicates whether the student's results for this quiz/exam should be displayed immediately upon submission, or later by admin release. This property is present only if 'contentType' is 'Quiz' or 'Exam'.

createdAt.description: The date and time when the course content was first created.

updatedAt.description: The date and time when the course content was last updated.

Question.title: Question
Question.description: Represents a single question that can be part of a Quiz or Exam (CourseContent).

id.description: Unique identifier for the Question entity.

courseContentId.description: Reference to the CourseContent (Quiz or Exam) this question belongs to. (Relationship: CourseContent 1:N Question)

questionText.description: The main text of the question.

questionImageUrl.description: URL to an image that visually represents or supplements the question (optional).

questionType.description: The type of question, either 'MCQ' (Multiple Choice Question) or 'Essay'.

points.description: The number of points awarded for correctly answering this question.

orderIndex.description: The sequential order of this question within its parent quiz or exam.

essayCorrectAnswerText.description: The reference text for the correct answer to an essay question, used by admins for grading. This property is present only if 'questionType' is 'Essay'.

essayCorrectAnswerFileUrl.description: URL to a PDF file containing the correct answer for an essay question, used by admins for grading. This property is present only if 'questionType' is 'Essay'.

QuestionOption.title: QuestionOption
QuestionOption.description: Represents an option for a Multiple-Choice Question (MCQ).

id.description: Unique identifier for the QuestionOption entity.

questionId.description: Reference to the Question this option belongs to. (Relationship: Question 1:N QuestionOption)

optionText.description: The text content of the multiple-choice option.

isCorrect.description: Indicates whether this option is the correct answer for the MCQ.

AccessCode.title: AccessCode
AccessCode.description: Represents a unique code generated by an admin to grant access to a specific course.

id.description: Unique identifier for the AccessCode entity.

code.description: The unique string value of the access code.

courseId.description: Reference to the Course that this access code unlocks. (Relationship: Course 1:N AccessCode)

generatedByAdminUserId.description: Reference to the AdminUser who generated this access code. (Relationship: AdminUser 1:N AccessCode)

createdAt.description: The date and time when the access code was generated.

isUsed.description: Indicates whether this access code has been used by a student.

usedByStudentId.description: Reference to the Student who used this access code. This property is present only if 'isUsed' is true. (Relationship: Student 1:1 AccessCode)

usedAt.description: The date and time when the access code was used. This property is present only if 'isUsed' is true.

StudentEnrollment.title: StudentEnrollment
StudentEnrollment.description: Records a student's enrollment in a specific course.

id.description: Unique identifier for the StudentEnrollment entity.

studentId.description: Reference to the Student who is enrolled. (Relationship: Student 1:N StudentEnrollment)

courseId.description: Reference to the Course the student is enrolled in. (Relationship: Course 1:N StudentEnrollment)

enrollmentDate.description: The date and time when the student officially enrolled in the course.

accessCodeId.description: Reference to the AccessCode used to facilitate this enrollment. (Relationship: AccessCode 1:1 StudentEnrollment)

isCompleted.description: Indicates whether the student has completed all required content for the course.

completionDate.description: The date and time when the student completed the course. This property is present only if 'isCompleted' is true.

progressPercentage.description: The current overall progress of the student in the course, represented as a percentage (0-100).

StudentVideoProgress.title: StudentVideoProgress
StudentVideoProgress.description: Tracks a student's viewing progress for a specific video content item within a course.

id.description: Unique identifier for the StudentVideoProgress entity.

studentId.description: Reference to the Student. (Relationship: Student 1:N StudentVideoProgress)

courseContentId.description: Reference to the CourseContent (which must be of 'Video' type). (Relationship: CourseContent 1:N StudentVideoProgress)

watchedDurationInSeconds.description: The total duration in seconds of the video that the student has watched.

isCompleted.description: Indicates whether the student has watched the entire video content.

lastWatchedAt.description: The date and time of the last recorded instance the student watched this video.

StudentQuizAttempt.title: StudentQuizAttempt
StudentQuizAttempt.description: Records a student's attempt at a specific quiz or exam (CourseContent).

id.description: Unique identifier for the StudentQuizAttempt entity.

studentId.description: Reference to the Student who made this attempt. (Relationship: Student 1:N StudentQuizAttempt)

courseContentId.description: Reference to the CourseContent (which must be of 'Quiz' or 'Exam' type). (Relationship: CourseContent 1:N StudentQuizAttempt)

attemptNumber.description: The sequential number of this attempt for the specific quiz/exam by the student (e.g., 1st, 2nd).

submittedAt.description: The date and time when the student submitted this quiz or exam attempt.

score.description: The total score obtained by the student in this attempt, if graded.

isGraded.description: Indicates whether all answers for this attempt have been graded (either automatically or by an admin).

gradeReleasedByAdminUserId.description: Reference to the AdminUser who released the grades for this attempt. This property is present if grades are released by an admin. (Relationship: AdminUser 1:N StudentQuizAttempt)

gradeReleaseDate.description: The date and time when the grades for this attempt were made visible to the student.

StudentAnswer.title: StudentAnswer
StudentAnswer.description: Stores a student's answer to a specific question within a quiz or exam attempt.

id.description: Unique identifier for the StudentAnswer entity.

studentQuizAttemptId.description: Reference to the StudentQuizAttempt this answer belongs to. (Relationship: StudentQuizAttempt 1:N StudentAnswer)

questionId.description: Reference to the Question that was answered. (Relationship: Question 1:N StudentAnswer)

mcqSelectedOptionId.description: Reference to the QuestionOption selected by the student, if the question type is 'MCQ'. (Relationship: QuestionOption 1:N StudentAnswer)

essayAnswerText.description: The student's submitted text answer for an essay question. This property is present if 'questionType' is 'Essay' and the answer is text-based.

essayAnswerFileUrl.description: URL to a PDF file uploaded by the student as an answer for an essay question. This property is present if 'questionType' is 'Essay' and the answer is file-based.

isCorrect.description: Indicates whether the student's answer is correct (auto-graded for MCQs, or admin-graded for essays).

scoreAchieved.description: The points achieved by the student for this specific answer (can be partial for essay questions).

adminFeedback.description: Text feedback provided by an admin for the student's answer, especially for essay questions.

definition.description: A sentinel collection used for database authorization. The existence of a document at `/admin_roles/{uid}` signifies that the user `uid` has administrator privileges. Used for quick admin role checks in security rules.
Stores individual admin profiles. `adminUserId` corresponds to `request.auth.uid`. Admins (based on `admin_roles`) can read/write their own profile, and other admins can read all admin profiles.

params.name: adminUserId
params.description: The unique identifier of the administrator, derived from `request.auth.uid`.

definition.description: Stores individual student profiles. `studentId` corresponds to `request.auth.uid`. Students can read/write their own profile; admins can read all student profiles.

params.name: studentId
params.description: The unique identifier of the student, derived from `request.auth.uid`.

definition.description: Main collection for educational courses. Publicly readable for browsing. Write access is restricted to administrators. Includes `uploadedByAdminUserId` for admin ownership and `targetAcademicYear` for student filtering.

params.name: courseId
params.description: The unique identifier for the course.

definition.description: Subcollection storing content items (videos, quizzes, exams) for a specific course. Admins have full CRUD access. Students can read if they are enrolled in the parent course. Includes denormalized `course_uploadedByAdminUserId` for authorization independence.

params.name: courseId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent course.

params.name: contentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the content item within the course.

definition.description: Subcollection storing questions for a specific quiz or exam (`CourseContent`). Admins have full CRUD access. Students can read during an active attempt if enrolled in the parent course. Includes denormalized `course_uploadedByAdminUserId`, `courseContent_contentType`, and `courseContent_courseId` for authorization independence and validation.

params.name: courseId
params.description: The unique identifier of the grand-parent course.

params.name: contentId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent course content (quiz/exam).

params.name: questionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the question.

definition.description: Subcollection storing options for a multiple-choice question. Admins have full CRUD access. Students can read during an active attempt if enrolled in the parent course. Includes denormalized `course_uploadedByAdminUserId`, `courseContent_contentType`, `courseContent_courseId`, and `question_questionType` for authorization independence and validation.

params.name: courseId
params.description: The unique identifier of the great-grand-parent course.

params.name: contentId
params.description: The unique identifier of the grand-parent course content (quiz/exam).

params.name: questionId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent question.

params.name: optionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the question option.

definition.description: Stores unique access codes generated by administrators to unlock courses. Admins have full CRUD access and can view all codes. Students can update a specific code's `isUsed` status when enrolling in a course.

params.name: accessCodeId
params.description: The unique identifier for the access code.

definition.description: Subcollection recording a specific student's enrollment in a course. `studentId` is implicit from the path. Students can read/write their own enrollments. Admins can read all student enrollments (via collection group queries). Includes denormalized `course_uploadedByAdminUserId` and `course_targetAcademicYear` for authorization independence.

params.name: studentId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent student.

params.name: enrollmentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the student's course enrollment.

definition.description: Subcollection tracking a student's viewing progress for a video content item. `studentId` is implicit from the path. Students can read/write their own progress. Admins can read all progress. Includes denormalized `courseId`, `courseContent_contentType`, `courseContent_durationInSeconds`, and `course_uploadedByAdminUserId` for authorization independence and validation.

params.name: studentId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent student.

params.name: progressId
params.description: The unique identifier for the video progress record.

definition.description: Subcollection recording a student's attempt at a quiz or exam. `studentId` is implicit from the path. Students can read/write their own attempts. Admins can read all attempts and write scores/feedback. Includes denormalized `courseId`, `courseContent_contentType`, `courseContent_passMarkPercentage`, `courseContent_allowInstantResultsDisplay`, and `course_uploadedByAdminUserId` for authorization independence and validation.

params.name: studentId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent student.

params.name: attemptId
params.description: The unique identifier for the student's quiz/exam attempt.

definition.description: Subcollection storing a student's answer to a specific question within a quiz or exam attempt. `studentId` and `attemptId` are implicit from the path. Students can read/write their own answers. Admins can read/write feedback and scores. Includes denormalized `courseId`, `courseContentId`, `questionId`, `questionType`, and `course_uploadedByAdminUserId` for authorization independence and validation.

params.name: studentId
params.description: The unique identifier of the great-grand-parent student.

params.name: attemptId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent quiz/exam attempt.

params.name: answerId
params.description: The unique identifier for the student's answer.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure is designed to be secure, scalable, and highly debuggable, strictly adhering to the core design principles and mandates. The core strategy revolves around **Authorization Independence through extensive denormalization** and **Structural Segregation** to ensure homogeneous security postures within collections.

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL for QAPs and atomic operations):**
To eliminate `get()` calls in security rules, especially for write operations, critical authorization context is denormalized directly into relevant subcollection documents. For instance:
*   `CourseContent` documents (videos, quizzes, exams) will include `course_uploadedByAdminUserId`. This allows rules to immediately verify if the `request.auth.uid` is the admin who uploaded the parent course when performing CRUD on content, without needing to `get()` the parent `/courses/{courseId}` document.
*   `Question` and `QuestionOption` documents denormalize `course_uploadedByAdminUserId`, `courseContent_contentType`, and `courseId`. This means that when a question or option is created/updated, rules can validate its context (e.g., ensure `contentType` is 'Quiz' or 'Exam' for questions) and ownership without retrieving parent documents.
*   Student-specific progress and attempt records (`StudentEnrollment`, `StudentVideoProgress`, `StudentQuizAttempt`, `StudentAnswer`) denormalize `courseId`, `courseContentId`, and `course_uploadedByAdminUserId` (and other relevant content metadata like `contentType`, `passMarkPercentage`). This is paramount: when a student attempts to update their video progress or submit a quiz answer, the rules can directly check if they are enrolled in the `courseId` associated with that content and if the content type is appropriate, all by inspecting the document being written and existing `StudentEnrollment` documents (which are user-specific and easy to query for `request.auth.uid`). This significantly simplifies security rules for write operations.

**Query as Authorization Patterns (QAPs) and Structural Segregation:**
*   **Public Access:** The `/courses/{courseId}` collection is publicly readable, allowing all users (including unauthenticated) to browse available courses. This satisfies QAP for 'listing latest courses on welcome page'.
*   **Admin-Specific Access:** Administrator roles are established using a dedicated top-level collection: `/admin_roles/{uid}`. The *existence* of a document at this path signifies admin status, enabling quick and efficient DBAC checks in rules (`exists(/databases/$(database)/documents/admin_roles/$(request.auth.uid))`). Admins have full CRUD access to all core platform data (`/courses`, `/access_codes`, and their subcollections). For student-specific data (e.g., all enrollments, all quiz attempts), admins will utilize **Collection Group Queries** across the respective subcollections (e.g., `enrollments`, `quiz_attempts`). This allows admins to list all such documents across all students, while students are restricted to their own data via path-based security.
*   **Student-Specific Access (Private Data):** All student-owned data is structured under `/students/{studentId}/...`. This leverages **Path-Based Ownership** (C.1 and C.2 mandates). For example, `StudentEnrollment` documents reside in `/students/{studentId}/enrollments/{enrollmentId}`. This design immediately enforces that a student (`request.auth.uid`) can only read/write documents where `{studentId}` in the path matches their `uid`. This makes `list` operations for students inherently secure and efficient, as they implicitly query only their own data.
*   **Course Content Access (Enrolled Students):** Access to `CourseContent` (and its nested `Questions`/`Options`) is granted to students who have an active enrollment in the parent course. Rules will verify the existence of a document in `/students/{request.auth.uid}/enrollments` that matches the `courseId` of the requested content. While this involves a `get()` call to check `StudentEnrollment`, it's a specific `get()` on authorization-relevant data for the current user, rather than a `get()` on a general parent document, and is primarily for read access. For write operations (e.g., updating video progress or submitting answers), the denormalized `courseId` within `StudentVideoProgress` or `StudentQuizAttempt` allows rules to cross-verify against the `StudentEnrollment` without further `get()` calls on the parent content.

This structure ensures clear separation of concerns, explicit authorization contexts, and highly efficient security rule evaluation, minimizing potential for authorization bypasses and improving system debuggability.
